halibut_spots_alameda: The Alameda Rock Wall stands out as a prime early-season spot with sandy bottoms ideal for halibut ambush.
halibut_spots_general: Homer claims the title of 'Halibut Capital of the World' due to landing more halibut than any other port over recent years, offering catches from Kachemak Bay to deep-water giants over 400 pounds.
striped_bass_spots_bay: The south tower is a common Striped Bass spot, as well as rocky structures off Alcatraz and Treasure Islands.
